name: player_contract_base_salary crafted year_offset range yields valid SQL and the real contract value (executed-result parity)
description: Seed one player with a 2024 player_contracts row (base_salary=5000.000). Before the year-less-source guard, a crafted year_offset:[1,2] emitted a correlated SUM over the player_contracts JOIN alias that skip_join_for_offset_range had already DROPPED -- invalid SQL (relation does not exist). The guard keeps the normal single-value join, so the query runs and returns the seeded base_salary. contract_year is pinned to 2024 to keep the fixture date-independent.
expected_query: select "player"."pid", player.fname, player.lname, "tc447a24b5421261899fec717107b6e9b"."base_salary" AS "base_salary_0", "player"."pos" from "player" left join "player_contracts" as "tc447a24b5421261899fec717107b6e9b" on "tc447a24b5421261899fec717107b6e9b"."pid" = "player"."pid" and tc447a24b5421261899fec717107b6e9b.year = '2024' where player.pos IN ('ZZ') group by player.fname, player.lname, "tc447a24b5421261899fec717107b6e9b"."base_salary", "player"."pid", "player"."lname", "player"."fname", "player"."pos" order by "player"."pid" asc limit 500

seed: INSERT INTO player (pid, fname, lname, pname, formatted, pos, pos1, dob, nfl_draft_year) VALUES ('TEST-CTRX-000900', 'Test', 'Ctrx', 'T.Ctrx', 'Test Ctrx', 'ZZ', 'ZZ', '2000-01-01', 2020)
INSERT INTO player_contracts (pid, year, team, base_salary) VALUES ('TEST-CTRX-000900', '2024', 'KC', 5000.000)
result_equivalence.reference_sql: SELECT base_salary AS base_salary_0 FROM player_contracts WHERE pid = 'TEST-CTRX-000900' AND year = '2024'
